content: Define the module interface including all input and output ports as specified in the original problem description. This includes setting up the module declaration with appropriate port directions and bit widths.
source: Implement the same interface and behavior as `original.txt`.

content: Implement the sequential logic for registering the input signal. This involves creating a register to store the input value and updating this register on the positive edge of the clock. Include conditions to handle synchronous reset.
source: The module should implement an 8-bit registered incrementer. The 8-bit input is first registered and then incremented by one on the next cycle.

content: Implement the combinational logic to increment the registered value. This logic will take the value from the input register, increment it by one, and pass it to the output.
source: The 8-bit input is first registered and then incremented by one on the next cycle.

content: Connect the output of the combinational logic to the module's output port. This involves assigning the incremented value to the output port so that it can be accessed externally.
source: The module should implement an 8-bit registered incrementer.
